patterns.provider_trait: Each provider implements the Provider trait (name/detect/fetch). detect() is sync, fetch() is async. Errors are returned as ProviderSnapshot with error field set, never propagated.
patterns.module_registration: Each provider is a separate module file (e.g., claude.rs) with `pub mod` in mod.rs. all_providers() returns Vec<Box<dyn Provider>> with all registered providers.
patterns.parse_separation: Network-dependent fetch() logic delegates to a pure parse_*_response() method that can be unit-tested without network calls. ClaudeProvider::parse_usage_response() is the reference pattern.
patterns.error_handling: All network/IO failures produce a ProviderSnapshot with error field set and None percentages rather than propagating anyhow::Error. This keeps cmd_providers resilient.
patterns.credentials_pattern: Credentials are read fresh on each fetch() call, never cached. File path via dirs::home_dir(). macOS Keychain as secondary source (Claude uses security CLI).
patterns.async_trait_usage: #[async_trait::async_trait] on impl blocks, async fn fetch(&self) -> anyhow::Result<ProviderSnapshot>
patterns.test_pattern: In-file #[cfg(test)] mod tests. Parse helpers tested with hardcoded JSON strings. detect() tested by temporarily setting HOME to temp dir.
patterns.http_client: reqwest::Client::new() created per-request inside fetch(). No persistent client. Features: json + rustls-tls.
patterns.binary_detection: ClaudeProvider only checks credential files/Keychain. Codex/Gemini need PATH detection — use std::process::Command::new("which").arg(binary) since no which crate in deps.

existing_implementations.description: Spec 030 established the Provider trait, ProviderSnapshot, ClaudeProvider, history JSONL, and bop providers CLI command. This spec adds CodexProvider and GeminiProvider following the same patterns.

already_available: tokio (1, rt-multi-thread + macros + time + process + signal + net)
